findings.title: `specflow-run start` still fails through the old git-root path before WorkspaceContext is injected
findings.detail: `main()` still calls `projectRoot()` before dispatching subcommands. Outside a git repo, `specflow-run start` exits with stderr `Error: not inside a git repository` instead of the required stdout JSON `{"status":"error","error":"not_in_git_repo"}`. This breaks Acceptance Criteria 5/6 and task 3.4/7.14. Fix by dispatching `start` before any raw git lookup, constructing `WorkspaceContext` first, and deriving the root/workflow/store paths from `ctx.projectRoot()`. Add an integration test for `specflow-run start` in a non-git directory.

findings.title: CLI pathspec filtering regressed
findings.detail: The new arg handling only extracts `:(exclude)...` entries and ignores all other pathspecs. That breaks the documented interface `specflow-filter-diff [-- <pathspec>...]`: for example, `specflow-filter-diff -- sub` now returns diffs for files outside `sub/` as well. This violates the no-breaking-change requirement and diff parity. Preserve non-exclude pathspecs and pass them through to diff selection, or explicitly reject unsupported args after updating the spec. Add a regression test covering an include pathspec.

findings.title: Invalid exclude-pattern warnings are lost on empty diffs
findings.detail: Before this refactor, `specflow-filter-diff` preserved `DIFF_EXCLUDE_PATTERNS` validation warnings even when there were no changes. Now `filteredDiff()` collapses all no-change cases to `summary: "empty"`, and the CLI synthesizes `warnings: []`, so `DIFF_EXCLUDE_PATTERNS='[' specflow-filter-diff` silently drops the invalid-pattern warning. That changes user-input error handling and violates the parity requirement for warning behavior. Return warnings alongside the empty result or have the CLI preserve the old warning-generation path. Add a test for invalid `DIFF_EXCLUDE_PATTERNS` with no repo changes.

findings.title: Unrelated runtime config change is bundled into the WorkspaceContext work
findings.detail: Adding `max_autofix_rounds: 6` changes repository behavior but is not described anywhere in the WorkspaceContext proposal. It should be removed from this diff or split into a separate spec/change so the implementation stays within scope.

findings.title: Apply review no longer skips excluded-only diffs
findings.detail: The old `diffFilter()` treated an empty patch body from `specflow-filter-diff` as `no_changes`, so apply review skipped when the worktree contained only deleted files / pure renames / other filtered-out tracked entries. After switching to `ctx.filteredDiff(...)`, `runReviewPipeline()` only short-circuits on `summary === "empty"`. For a deleted-file-only change, `filteredDiff()` returns `diff: ""` with a non-empty summary, so `specflow-review-apply review` now calls the reviewer on an empty diff and can write a success ledger/current-phase update even though there are no reviewable changes. Restore the old behavior by treating `rawDiff.diff === ""` as `no_changes` (or normalizing that case inside `diffFilter()`), and add a regression test for an excluded-only diff such as a deleted file.

findings.title: Pathspecs are now resolved from the repo root instead of the caller's cwd
findings.detail: The old implementation ran `git diff` in `process.cwd()`, so `specflow-filter-diff -- file.txt` worked when invoked from a nested directory like `repo/sub`. The refactor now calls `filterLocalWorkspaceDiff(ctx.projectRoot(), { pathspecs: args })`, which reroots the command to the repository root and turns that same invocation into an empty diff. Preserve the original cwd when evaluating user pathspecs, and add a regression test that runs the CLI from a subdirectory.

findings.title: `specflow-run start` changes the existing non-git error output contract
findings.detail: Before this refactor, running `specflow-run start` outside a git repo failed with the standard stderr message `Error: not inside a git repository`. The new `start` branch now emits `{"status":"error","error":"not_in_git_repo"}` on stdout with empty stderr, and the added test locks in that behavior. That does not satisfy the proposal's `No CLI breaking change` acceptance criterion. Restore the previous error surface, or explicitly update the requirement and all consumers if the JSON error is intentional.
